content:
===BEGIN===
I was at the park with my kids when I noticed the sharp crackle in the air and the smell of ozone—signs of lightning nearby. I pulled my kids away just as a bolt struck the tree we’d been standing under. Then, I felt it—an intense jolt, like every nerve was on fire. I’d been hit.

I expected pain, maybe worse. Instead, something strange happened. Colors grew vivid, sounds crisp and layered. I could hear the rustle of leaves a block away, the faint hum of a bee on a flower, even the subtle shift of the wind against my skin. My senses had somehow amplified—one hundred times over, it seemed.

Skeptical? I was too. But this wasn’t hype or fantasy. It was real. And now, with senses heightened, I’m beginning to see the world—not as I thought it was—but as it truly is.
